templateMeta.name: Content Template
templateMeta.description: Generalized content artifact producer. Takes any combination of sources (URLs, text, references), a lookback period, and a content brief — produces a labeled content artifact. Sources are researched in parallel and synthesized into original content.

name.description: Human label for this content run (e.g., 'Weekly AI Digest', 'Market Brief')

sources.description: Things to check for content — URLs, text, references. The agent decides how to use each.

lookbackPeriod.description: How far back to look for content (e.g., '7 days', '24 hours', '30 days')

contentBrief.description: What angle or purpose the content should serve — the substance guidance

formatBrief.description: Style and formatting guidance — tone (formal/casual), structure (e.g., '3 paragraphs'), length preferences, voice

fields.name: sourcesChecked
fields.description: Number of sources that were checked for content

fields.name: sourcesCited
fields.description: Number of sources actually cited in the output

invariants.condition: You research every source in {{sources}} for content relevant to the brief, considering only material from within {{lookbackPeriod}} relative to {{currentTimestamp}}. Each source should be thoroughly investigated — follow leads, check related material, and build a picture of what's happened.
invariants.assessment: Every source in the input array was checked. Research artifacts exist with findings and citations. Material outside the lookback window is excluded unless it provides essential context.

do: Fetch a URL source and scan for recent developments within the lookback period
Use a text source as context to guide web research on the topic
Follow leads discovered during research — a source mentions a report, go find that report
Create structured findings artifacts so synthesis has material to work with
dont: Ignore sources from the input array
Include stale content from well outside the lookback period without justification
Skim a source superficially when deeper investigation would yield better material

invariants.condition: You synthesize research findings into content following the brief: {{contentBrief}}. Content must be original synthesis — group related findings thematically, cite specific sources, and produce something more valuable than any individual source alone.
invariants.assessment: The output content addresses the brief's angle/purpose. Findings are synthesized into a coherent narrative, not concatenated. Specific sources are cited. The content adds value beyond what the sources individually provide.

do: Connect findings across sources to surface patterns or insights
Cite specific sources when making claims
Organize content by theme, not by source
Produce original framing that helps the reader understand the landscape
dont: Ignore the content brief's stated angle or purpose

invariants.condition: You follow the style and formatting guidance: {{formatBrief}}. The tone, structure, and presentation of the content match what was requested.
invariants.assessment: The output content matches the requested tone (formal/casual/technical), follows the requested structure (paragraph count, section layout), and respects any length or voice preferences specified in the format brief.

do: If formatBrief says 'casual tone, 3 short paragraphs' — write exactly that
If formatBrief says 'formal research report with executive summary' — structure accordingly
Match the voice consistently throughout the piece
dont: Ignore formatting instructions and default to a generic style
Mix tones inconsistently within the same piece
Produce a 2000-word essay when the brief asks for a concise summary

invariants.assessment: Rate the output 0–100: Relevance to brief (25%) — does the content address the stated purpose? Source coverage (25%) — were the provided sources meaningfully used? Synthesis quality (25%) — is this more than the sum of its parts? Readability (25%) — is it clear, well-structured, and appropriate for the audience?
